scenarios.name: simple_crud_feature
scenarios.prompt: We discussed adding a notes feature to portfolios. Users can create, edit, delete notes attached to a portfolio. Notes are markdown, max 10k chars.
scenarios.context: Django REST backend, React frontend, existing Portfolio model

scenarios.name: complex_integration
scenarios.prompt: We need to integrate with Plaid for bank account verification. Users link their bank, we pull transaction history nightly, and surface it in a dashboard.
scenarios.context: Python backend, existing user auth system, no current third-party integrations

scenarios.name: refactor_existing
scenarios.prompt: The reporting module is a mess. We want to extract it into a clean service that other parts of the app can call. Currently it's spread across 4 controllers.
scenarios.context: Existing monolith with CONTEXT.md defining domain terms, docs/adr/ with 3 existing ADRs

scenarios.name: frontend_only
scenarios.prompt: Add a dark mode toggle to the settings page. Should persist preference and apply globally.
scenarios.context: React app with Tailwind CSS, no existing theme system

scenarios.name: data_pipeline
scenarios.prompt: Build an ETL pipeline that ingests CSV uploads from LPs, validates schema, normalizes data, and loads into our fund_metrics table. Must handle partial failures.
scenarios.context: Python, existing PostgreSQL schema, CONTEXT.md with LP/fund terminology

assertions.name: no_interviewing
assertions.description: Output synthesizes from existing context. Does not ask the user discovery questions to gather requirements.

rubric.0: Output is entirely questions asking user what they want
rubric.25: Output asks multiple discovery questions before producing content
rubric.50: Output produces some PRD content but intersperses questions gathering new requirements
rubric.75: Output synthesizes from context, asks only the required module-check and write-location questions
rubric.100: Output synthesizes entirely from context, only asks the two mandated process questions (module check + write location)

assertions.name: explores_repo
assertions.description: Output demonstrates awareness of codebase structure, existing patterns, or domain vocabulary.

rubric.0: No mention of codebase, existing code, or project structure
rubric.25: Generic mention of 'exploring the repo' without any specific findings
rubric.50: References some codebase elements but superficially, no real analysis
rubric.75: Demonstrates awareness of specific patterns, models, or architecture in the codebase
rubric.100: Deep codebase awareness shown through specific references to existing patterns, models, services, and how the new feature fits

assertions.name: domain_vocabulary
assertions.description: Output uses project-specific terminology from CONTEXT.md/ADRs when available. When unavailable, explicitly notes their absence and uses best domain terms from conversation context.

rubric.0: Uses only generic software terms, no domain language, does not check for CONTEXT.md/ADRs
rubric.25: Checks for CONTEXT.md/ADRs but does not note their absence or use any domain terms
rubric.50: Uses some domain terms but does not check for or note the status of CONTEXT.md/ADRs
rubric.75: When CONTEXT.md/ADRs exist: uses them consistently. When they do not exist: explicitly notes their absence and uses best available domain terms from conversation context
rubric.100: All domain concepts use project-specific terminology from CONTEXT.md/ADRs throughout the PRD. Or, if none exist, explicitly notes absence and uses rich domain language from conversation context with a note in Further Notes

assertions.name: sketches_modules
assertions.description: Output identifies major modules to build/modify and discusses deep module extraction opportunities.

rubric.50: Lists modules but no discussion of interfaces or deep module extraction
rubric.75: Identifies specific modules with interfaces, but no deep module analysis
rubric.100: Identifies modules, discusses interfaces, and explicitly looks for deep module extraction opportunities

assertions.name: asks_write_location
assertions.description: Output asks the user where to write the PRD (current branch, spike, staged) before writing.

rubric.0: Writes PRD without asking, or never mentions file location
rubric.25: Mentions writing but does not ask the user to choose a location
rubric.50: Asks where to write but does not offer the three specific options (current branch, spike, staged)
rubric.75: Asks where to write with the three options but does not wait for user response before proceeding
rubric.100: Explicitly asks where to write with all three options (current branch, new spike branch, new staged branch) and waits for user choice

assertions.name: derives_slug
assertions.description: Output derives a kebab-case slug from the feature name, max ~40 chars.

rubric.25: Mentions a filename but not in kebab-case or derived from feature name
rubric.50: Derives a slug but wrong format (camelCase, snake_case, or over 40 chars)
rubric.75: Correct kebab-case slug derived from feature name, reasonable length
rubric.100: Correct kebab-case slug, clearly derived from feature name, under 40 chars, used consistently in file path

assertions.name: correct_file_prefix
assertions.description: Output determines file prefix by scanning docs/prd/ for existing NNNN pattern.

rubric.25: Uses a hardcoded prefix without scanning
rubric.50: Mentions scanning docs/prd/ but does not describe the NNNN incrementing logic
rubric.75: Describes scanning for NNNN pattern and incrementing, but minor errors in zero-padding or edge cases
rubric.100: Correctly describes scanning docs/prd/ for NNNN-*.md, taking highest + 1, zero-padded to 4 digits, defaulting to 0001

assertions.name: template_compliance
assertions.description: PRD contains all required sections: Problem Statement, Solution, User Stories, Implementation Decisions, Testing Decisions, Out of Scope, Further Notes.

rubric.75: Has 5-6 of the 7 required sections, or all 7 but some are empty or trivial
rubric.100: All 7 sections present with substantive content in each

assertions.name: user_story_format
assertions.description: User stories follow 'As a X, I want Y, so that Z' format. List is extensive (5+).

rubric.25: User stories exist but wrong format, or fewer than 3
rubric.75: 5+ user stories in correct format but some are vague or redundant
rubric.100: 5+ user stories in correct 'As a X, I want Y, so that Z' format, each distinct and covering different aspects

assertions.name: no_file_paths_in_decisions
assertions.description: Implementation Decisions section avoids specific file paths or code snippets.

rubric.0: Implementation Decisions is full of file paths and code snippets
rubric.25: Multiple file paths or code blocks in Implementation Decisions
rubric.50: 1-2 file paths or small code references in Implementation Decisions
rubric.75: No file paths but includes minor code-like references (function signatures, etc.)
rubric.100: Implementation Decisions contains only architectural and design decisions, no file paths or code

assertions.name: suggests_to_tasks
assertions.description: Output tells user the file path and suggests running /to-tasks next.

rubric.0: No mention of next steps or file path
rubric.25: Mentions file path but no next step suggestion
rubric.50: Mentions next steps but does not specifically suggest /to-tasks
rubric.75: Suggests /to-tasks but does not mention the file path
rubric.100: Clearly states the PRD file path and suggests running /to-tasks next
